title: 106 likes, 2 comments - womensheartalliance on December 13, 2023: "Heart disease is the #1 killer of all women, and 80%
content: 106 likes, 2 comments - womensheartalliance on December 13, 2023: "Heart disease is the #1 killer of all women, and 80% of the time it is preventable. . Take control of your #HeartHealth by recognizing and adopting habits you can control, including exercising and meditating to cut down on stress—it may just save your life! . Check out our website for more facts and resources. . .
summary: 106 likes, 2 comments - womensheartalliance on December 13, 2023: "Heart disease is the #1 killer of all women, and 80% of the time it is preventable.
author: Women's Heart Alliance

title: 32 likes, 0 comments - womensheartalliance on September 10, 2025: "#BayerPartner Make sure the women in your life know h
content: 32 likes, 0 comments - womensheartalliance on September 10, 2025: "#BayerPartner Make sure the women in your life know how important it is to understand their heart disease risk factors. Why? Because it kills more of us than all cancers combined. Because it leaves loved ones behind. Women’s Heart Alliance encourages you to share a quick & easy 2-minute assessment from @bayeraspirin_us with a friend or loved one so they can learn about their potential risk factors for heart disease—it’ll do your heart good (and hers). #GetHeartChecked
summary: 32 likes, 0 comments - womensheartalliance on September 10, 2025: "#BayerPartner Make sure the women in your life know how important it is to understand their heart disease risk factors.

title: 104 likes, 7 comments - womensheartalliance on February 3, 2025: "Women’s symptoms for heart disease are far too often d
content: 104 likes, 7 comments - womensheartalliance on February 3, 2025: "Women’s symptoms for heart disease are far too often dismissed or ignored. . In fact, our recent survey results revealed that a shocking 84% of cardiologists reported treating women whose symptoms were misdiagnosed in the last year. . Join us in the fight for improved awareness and more heart health research that is focused on women! . Visit the comments section below to learn more facts and how you can protect yourself against heart disease. . .
summary: 104 likes, 7 comments - womensheartalliance on February 3, 2025: "Women’s symptoms for heart disease are far too often dismissed or ignored.
author: Women's Heart Alliance

title: 69 likes, 2 comments - womensheartalliance on February 12, 2025: "Heart disease doesn’t discriminate, but research does.
content: 69 likes, 2 comments - womensheartalliance on February 12, 2025: "Heart disease doesn’t discriminate, but research does. . Women make up only 35% of trial participants & fewer studies report outcomes by sex. How can this be? . It’s time we unite and demand fair representation for women in health research—together, we can close the gap and save lives! . Visit the link in the comments section below to learn more. . .
summary: 69 likes, 2 comments - womensheartalliance on February 12, 2025: "Heart disease doesn’t discriminate, but research does.
author: Women's Heart Alliance

title: 36 likes, 0 comments - womensheartalliance on May 10, 2024: "It’s not “all in your head”!
content: 36 likes, 0 comments - womensheartalliance on May 10, 2024: "It’s not “all in your head”! . Here are the facts: #HeartDisease is the No. 1 killer of women and is widely underdiagnosed and underrated. . This #MothersDay, be a force for change. Watch and share the PSA linked in our bio to learn the unique symptoms of heart disease in women. . .
summary: 36 likes, 0 comments - womensheartalliance on May 10, 2024: "It’s not “all in your head”!
author: Women's Heart Alliance

title: 47 likes, 2 comments - womensheartalliance on February 14, 2025: "Heart disease is the No.
content: 47 likes, 2 comments - womensheartalliance on February 14, 2025: "Heart disease is the No. 1 killer of women, but it doesn’t have to be. . Knowing the signs and symptoms is the first step to protecting yourself and leading a heart healthy life—before it’s too late. . This #HeartMonth, get heart checked and help us save lives by sharing these facts with the women you love. . Learn the facts and find the resources you need to help protect yourself against #HeartDisease, visit the link in the bio today! . .
summary: 47 likes, 2 comments - womensheartalliance on February 14, 2025: "Heart disease is the No.

title: 55 likes, 3 comments - womensheartalliance on January 31, 2025: "Cardiovascular disease (CVD) is the leading cause of de
content: 55 likes, 3 comments - womensheartalliance on January 31, 2025: "Cardiovascular disease (CVD) is the leading cause of death for women, yet women are often overlooked when it comes to #HeartHealth. This needs to change! . Join us in our fight to close the gap in heart care for women and ensure they have access to the care and resources they deserve. . Visit the link in the comments section below to learn more! . .

title: 44 likes, 0 comments - womensheartalliance on May 12, 2024: "Missed or delayed diagnosis of heart disease can impact wom
content: 44 likes, 0 comments - womensheartalliance on May 12, 2024: "Missed or delayed diagnosis of heart disease can impact women of all ages. And too often women, especially mothers, take care of everyone else before themselves. . This #MothersDay, be a force for change! Help mothers and the women in your life by sharing the symptoms of #HeartDisease that they should watch out for. . . .

title: 18 likes, 0 comments - womensheartalliance on September 8, 2025: "#BayerPartner Close to half the women in the United St
content: 18 likes, 0 comments - womensheartalliance on September 8, 2025: "#BayerPartner Close to half the women in the United States are living with some form of heart disease, and every year it kills more of us than all cancers combined—that’s 446,912 of our mothers, grandmothers, daughters, sisters, and friends lost to a disease that’s mostly preventable. Yet heart disease is all too often missed, dismissed, or misdiagnosed, and women pay the price. It doesn’t have to be like this. Women’s Heart Alliance encourages you to take an easy 2-minute assessment from @bayeraspirin_us to understand your personal risk factors for heart disease, and bring the results to a doctor. You’ll do your heart good. #GetHeartChecked
